failure_modes.text: Teams can call every preferred initiative a leverage point without proving that it sits near a real rule, bottleneck, or feedback loop.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when leaders label every preferred initiative a leverage point without proving that it sits near a real feedback loop, bottleneck, or governing rule.
failure_modes.mitigation: Require proof that the proposed leverage point sits near a real rule, bottleneck, or feedback loop; labeling a preferred initiative as leverage is not evidence.

failure_modes.text: Ocean-boiling analysis can bury the few structural moves that actually matter.
failure_modes.mitigation: Focus analysis on the few structural moves that actually matter; ocean-boiling analysis that maps everything buries the signal.

failure_modes.text: A good leverage diagnosis can still fail if the execution gap between what to do and how to do it is ignored.
failure_modes.source_quote: It is often straightforward to identify the necessary strategy (the "what," or the leverage point), but extremely difficult to figure out the execution (the "how").
failure_modes.mitigation: Close the execution gap: a good leverage diagnosis still fails if the team does not design how to implement the structural move.

premortem_questions.text: How does what you’re doing solve the problem?
premortem_questions.source_quote: How does what you’re doing solve the problem?

premortem_questions.text: How does it advance your thinking?
premortem_questions.source_quote: How does it advance your thinking?

premortem_questions.text: Is it the most important thing you could be doing right now?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Is it the most important thing you could be doing right now?

heuristics.text: Disaggregate the problem and prioritize the few structural elements that matter most before analysis expands.
Gather only enough facts to prove or disprove the central hypothesis instead of boiling the ocean.
heuristics.source_quote: To avoid wasting time on low-leverage activities ("Don't boil the ocean"), one must gather only enough facts required to prove or disprove a central hypothesis.

heuristics.text: Use value-driver trees to expose the few variables that truly move the system and make what-if analysis tractable.
heuristics.source_quote: Employing a Return on Capital Tree can expose the fundamental levers of the business.

curation_notes.summary: Focused on intervention prioritization, anti-ocean-boiling discipline, and the gap between identifying leverage and executing it.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor scoring or topology abstractions because the raw source is stronger on disaggregation, hypothesis focus, and structural levers.
open_questions: The third heuristic uses the value-driver tree example as a general intervention-selection tactic rather than preserving business-finance specificity.

allies: The raw allies section is strong on first-principles-thinking and structured decomposition for later relation preservation.
antagonists: The raw antagonists section is strongest on single-tool capture and expert abstraction, but only the cleanest current-vocabulary ids should survive Wave 3.
structured_tensions: The source contains an explicit structured tension block naming optionality, margin-of-safety, and confirmation-bias.
